title: eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. bocconei
intro: Eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. bocconei, also known as Sea Holly, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its thistle-like flowers and prickly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids content. The health benefits of Eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. bocconei are said to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
